name: the primitive type called bib

VINIT.justification: the raw data space before local constraints are applied

TANCH.justification: the limit of the mesoscale beyond which correlations become collective

AFWD.justification: projection of data into a local coordinate system

AREV.justification: mapping local coordinates back to the global field

CLINK.justification: sequential linking of adjacent local points

IMSCRIB.justification: recognition of a point as its own center within the bib scope

FSPLIT.justification: splitting a signal into correlated and uncorrelated components

FFUSE.justification: recombining components to recover the original local signal

EVALT.justification: successful identification of a local pattern

EVALF.justification: failure to find local structure within the bib radius

ENGAGR.justification: a state where a point belongs to two adjacent mesoscale regions

IFIX.justification: permanent fixation of the bib scale in the type registry

steps.domain_action: initialize the raw field for granularity assignment
project the field into a local coordinate frame
identify the central point of the bib neighborhood
branch the local signal into pattern and noise
confirm the presence of short-range correlations
reconstitute the original local signal from the branches
chain the local signal to adjacent mesoscale neighbors
hold the overlap between neighboring bib regions
record the bib granularity as a fixed type attribute
close the system within the mesoscale boundary

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the partitioning is a reversible logical assignment
